page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM. Powered by an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz, the instance allocates 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU architecture includes an L3 cache of 105 MB, alongside L1d and L2 caches of 48 KB and 2 MB respectively. This instance does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and it operates without a GPU.

Benchmark results place the c3-highcpu-8 in the average performance tier for most compute and memory operations. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1728.0 and a multi-core score of 6628.0, matching its stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 6628.05. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 544.86 GB/sec, which drops to 52.52 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which assist in workloads like cryptography and low-precision integer operations.

Tradeoffs are evident in multi-threaded decompression and larger model inference. While single-threaded gzip compression reaches 26.37 GB/sec, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is relatively weak at 35.86 GB/sec. Similarly, while prompt processing for a 7B LLM model reaches 51.04 tokens/sec, text generation on the same model drops to a weak 7.72 tokens/sec. Qualitatively, the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers cost efficiency for compute-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints, such as web serving, software compilation, and basic database operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM on an x86_64 architecture. Powered by a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8481C processor with 4 physical cores and hyper-threading, it provides dedicated CPU allocation and a 105 MB L3 cache. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs. Performance benchmarks generally fall within the average tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6628.0 and a static web serving throughput of 13.01 GB/sec. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX for cryptography and vector math. However, tradeoffs include weak multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and low text generation speeds on 7B LLM models. This instance is configured for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, web serving, and database operations that require a lean memory footprint.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8481C processor, designed for compute-heavy workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-8 is an 8 vCPU, 16 GB RAM compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for lean, CPU-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM on an Intel Xeon 8481C processor.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for cryptography and vector operations.
Average performance in software compilation, web serving, and database operations.
No local storage or bundled GPU resources.
Weak performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and 7B LLM text generation.
